channel.title: Daily AI Digest
channel.description: Curated AI/ML signals — zero LLMs, pure algorithmic curation

item.description: 💬 Major HN discussion (168 comments)

item.title: Ask HN: What Are You Working On? (April 2026)
item.description: 💬 Major HN discussion (663 comments)

item.title: CT-1: Vision-Language-Camera Models Transfer Spatial Reasoning Knowledge to Camera-Controllable Video Generation
item.description: 💬 Active HN discussion (56 comments) · 📄 Featured on Hugging Face Daily Papers — Camera-controllable video generation aims to synthesize videos with flexible and physically plausible camera movements. However, existing methods either provide imprecise camera control from text prompts or rely on labor-intensive manual camera trajectory parameters, limiting their use in automat...

item.title: WildDet3D: Scaling Promptable 3D Detection in the Wild
item.description: 📄 Featured on Hugging Face Daily Papers — Understanding objects in 3D from a single image is a cornerstone of spatial intelligence. A key step toward this goal is monocular 3D object detection--recovering the extent, location, and orientation of objects from an input RGB image. To be practical in the open world, such a detector must gene...

item.title: ELT: Elastic Looped Transformers for Visual Generation
item.description: 📄 Featured on Hugging Face Daily Papers — We introduce Elastic Looped Transformers (ELT), a highly parameter-efficient class of visual generative models based on a recurrent transformer architecture. While conventional generative models rely on deep stacks of unique transformer layers, our approach employs iterative, weight-shared transf...

item.title: Large Language Models Generate Harmful Content Using a Distinct, Unified Mechanism
item.description: 📄 New in cs.CL, cs.AI — Large language models (LLMs) undergo alignment training to avoid harmful behaviors, yet the resulting safeguards remain brittle: jailbreaks routinely bypass them, and fine-tuning on narrow domains can induce ``emergent misalignment'' that generalizes broadly. Whether this brittleness reflects a f...

item.title: VisionFoundry: Teaching VLMs Visual Perception with Synthetic Images
item.description: 📄 New in cs.CV, cs.AI — Vision-language models (VLMs) still struggle with visual perception tasks such as spatial understanding and viewpoint recognition. One plausible contributing factor is that natural image datasets provide limited supervision for low-level visual skills. This motivates a practical question: can tar...

item.title: ECHO: Efficient Chest X-ray Report Generation with One-step Block Diffusion
item.description: 📄 New in cs.LG, cs.AI — Chest X-ray report generation (CXR-RG) has the potential to substantially alleviate radiologists' workload. However, conventional autoregressive vision--language models (VLMs) suffer from high inference latency due to sequential token decoding. Diffusion-based models offer a promising alternative...

item.title: XFED: Non-Collusive Model Poisoning Attack Against Byzantine-Robust Federated Classifiers
item.description: 📄 New in cs.CR, cs.AI — Model poisoning attacks pose a significant security threat to Federated Learning (FL). Most existing model poisoning attacks rely on collusion, requiring adversarial clients to coordinate by exchanging local benign models and synchronizing the generation of their poisoned updates. However, sustai...

item.title: Is More Data Worth the Cost? Dataset Scaling Laws in a Tiny Attention-Only Decoder
item.description: 📄 New in cs.LG, cs.CL — Training Transformer language models is expensive, as performance typically improves with increasing dataset size and computational budget. Although scaling laws describe this trend at large scale, their implications in controlled, smaller-scale settings remain less explored. In this work, we iso...

item.title: Plasticity-Enhanced Multi-Agent Mixture of Experts for Dynamic Objective Adaptation in UAVs-Assisted Emergency Communication Networks
item.description: 📄 New in cs.MA, cs.LG — Unmanned aerial vehicles serving as aerial base stations can rapidly restore connectivity after disasters, yet abrupt changes in user mobility and traffic demands shift the quality of service trade-offs and induce strong non-stationarity. Deep reinforcement learning policies suffer from plasticit...

item.title: Hierarchical Kernel Transformer: Multi-Scale Attention with an Information-Theoretic Approximation Analysis
item.description: 📄 New in cs.LG, cs.NE — The Hierarchical Kernel Transformer (HKT) is a multi-scale attention mechanism that processes sequences at L resolution levels via trainable causal downsampling, combining level-specific score matrices through learned convex weights. The total computational cost is bounded by 4/3 times that of st...

item.title: NousResearch / hermes-agent
item.description: ⭐ 7.5k GitHub stars today · ⭐ 72.6k total stars — The agent that grows with you

item.title: HKUDS / DeepTutor
item.description: ⭐ 670 GitHub stars today · ⭐ 17.5k total stars — "DeepTutor: Agent-Native Personalized Learning Assistant"

item.title: ZhuLinsen / daily_stock_analysis
item.description: ⭐ 188 GitHub stars today · ⭐ 29.6k total stars — LLM驱动的 A/H/美股智能分析器：多数据源行情 + 实时新闻 + LLM决策仪表盘 + 多渠道推送，零成本定时运行，纯白嫖. LLM-powered stock analysis system for A/H/US markets.

item.title: LangChain Deep Dive: Building Modular LLM Applications from Scratch (With Code)

item.title: karpathy / micrograd
item.description: ⭐ 15.4k total stars — A tiny scalar-valued autograd engine and a neural net library on top of it with PyTorch-like API

item.title: patchy631 / ai-engineering-hub
item.description: ⭐ 48 stars today on GitHub · ⭐ 33.5k total stars — In-depth tutorials on LLMs, RAGs and real-world AI agent applications.
